message:
perf: wire f32x4.relaxed_madd into int4 matmul MAC loop (FMA)

Zig's @mulAdd(@Vector(4, f32), ...) falls back to scalar per-lane
software FMA on wasm, defeating relaxed-SIMD. Clang's
__builtin_wasm_relaxed_madd_f32x4 emits the opcode directly, so add
a tiny C helper (src/c/fma.c, always_inline) and link-time-inline
it into the Zig matmul via `-flto` on both sides.

288 f32x4.relaxed_madd ops now live in dist/pii.wasm; every MAC in
the int4 matmul hot path is fused (was mul + add).

Browser bench:
  short  71 → 66 ms   (0.40× → 0.46× vs tjs)
  medium 172 → 160 ms (0.22× → 0.24×)
  long   374 → 347 ms (0.15× → 0.16×)

Cumulative since the ONNX pivot: 211/579/1341 → 66/160/347 ms
(3.2–3.9× faster). Requires a runtime with `relaxed_simd` (V8 yes,
Node ≥ 21 yes, Bun not yet).

Parity: cross-backend smoke still 2/2 spans agree with tjs WebGPU;
public-api.spec.ts green.

files.patch:
@@ -38,7 +38,7 @@
   "license": "Apache-2.0",
   "scripts": {
     "build": "npm run build:zig && npm run build:opt && node scripts/inline-wasm.mjs && bun build src/js/index.ts --outdir dist --target browser --format esm --sourcemap=external && bun x tsc --emitDeclarationOnly",
-    "build:zig": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd -O ReleaseFast -fstrip src/zig/wasm_exports.zig --name pii -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv pii.wasm dist/",
+    "build:zig": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd -O ReleaseFast -fstrip -flto src/zig/wasm_exports.zig -cflags -O3 -mrelaxed-simd -flto -- src/c/fma.c --name pii -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv pii.wasm dist/",
     "build:opt": "wasm-opt -O3 --strip-producers --strip-target-features dist/pii.wasm -o dist/pii.wasm --enable-simd --enable-bulk-memory --enable-nontrapping-float-to-int --enable-sign-ext --enable-mutable-globals --enable-relaxed-simd",
     "typecheck": "bun x tsc --noEmit",
     "test": "bun x playwright test",

@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+// Wrap wasm's `f32x4.relaxed_madd` so Zig can call it. Zig's
+// `@mulAdd(@Vector(4, f32), ...)` falls back to per-lane software
+// FMA on wasm, defeating the whole point. Clang's builtin emits the
+// relaxed-SIMD instruction directly when the target has
+// +relaxed_simd. Built with -flto so the Zig/C link-time optimizer
+// inlines this one-instruction body into every matmul MAC.
+
+#include <wasm_simd128.h>
+
+__attribute__((always_inline)) v128_t
+relaxed_madd_f32x4(v128_t a, v128_t b, v128_t c) {
+    return __builtin_wasm_relaxed_madd_f32x4(a, b, c);
+}
